rulings.Case Title: SARAH LEWIS VS. KENNETH RUNYON ET AL
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion / Discovery calendar for April 20, 2026, line 1. PLAINTIFF SARAH LEWIS'S MOTION TO QUASH SUBPOENAS. Plaintiff Sarah Lewis' motion to quash deposition subpoenas is continued to April 27, 2026 to give Ms. Lewis an opportunity to provide the court with copies of the subpoenas that she seeks to have quashed. Without reviewing the subpoenas, the court is unable to decide this motion. No later than April 20, 2026 Ms. Lewis must provide copies of the subpoenas to department 301 with a cover letter stating the continued hearing date. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: VELOCITY INVESTMENTS, LLC VS. MAURICE MOSLEY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Vacate Judgment
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion / Discovery calendar for April 20, 2026, line 3. DEFENDANT MAURICE MOSLEY'S Motion To Vacate Judgment. Defendant Maurice Mosly's motion to vacate judgment is off calendar without prejudice to being refiled in accordance with the time requirements of Code of Civil Procedure 1005(b). For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: WELLS FARGO BANK, NA VS. ANTHONY DANIEL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION / Notice Of Motion & Motion For An Order Deeming The Truth Of The Matters Specified In Plaintiffs Request For Admissions As Admitted
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion / Discovery calendar for April 20, 2026, line 4. PLAINTIFF WELLS FARGO BANK, NA'S Motion For An Order Deeming The Truth Of The Matters Specified In Plaintiffs Request For Admissions As Admitted. Plaintiff Wells Fargo Bank, NA's motion for an order deeming admitted its requests for admission is granted. Because defendant Anthony Daniel has not served any responses to Wells Fargo's requests for admission, those requests are deemed admitted. No opposition filed. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.)
